_metadata.description: Complete Nacha ACH return code and NOC (Notification of Change) code reference. Return codes R01-R85 and NOC codes C01-C09. Each entry includes the code, description, return window (banking days), who may return it (RDFI or ODFI), Reg E implications, and whether it qualifies for unauthorized return processing.

R01.consumer_protection_notes: Not a consumer protection return. Originator may re-present up to 2 times after R01.

R02.consumer_protection_notes: Account no longer active. Originator should obtain new account information.

R03.description: No Account / Unable to Locate Account
R03.consumer_protection_notes: Account number does not correspond to an open account. Originator should verify account details.

R04.consumer_protection_notes: Account number fails check digit or format validation.

R05.consumer_protection_notes: Debit entry to a corporate account not authorized. Extended 60-day return window applies. Reg E may apply if entry was to a consumer account.

R07.consumer_protection_notes: Consumer revoked authorization after initially providing it. 60-day window. Reg E § 1005.10(c) governs stop payment rights. Strong Reg E dispute indicator.

R08.consumer_protection_notes: Consumer requested stop payment. Reg E § 1005.10(c) governs stop payment rights for preauthorized transfers. RDFI must honor stop payment within 3 business days of oral request.

R09.consumer_protection_notes: Funds exist but are not yet collected (check hold). Originator may re-present.

R10.description: Customer Advises Not Authorized / Originator Not Known
R10.consumer_protection_notes: Consumer disputes authorization entirely. Strongest Reg E unauthorized transaction return. 60-day extended window. RDFI must obtain written statement from consumer. Investigation required.

R11.description: Customer Advises Entry Not in Accordance With Authorization
R11.consumer_protection_notes: Authorization existed but charge differs from authorized terms (wrong amount, wrong date, etc.). Reg E incorrect amount dispute. 60-day window.

R13.consumer_protection_notes: Routing number not in Federal Reserve routing directory.

R14.description: Representative Payee Deceased or Unable to Continue in That Capacity
R14.consumer_protection_notes: Benefits payment to representative payee whose authority has ended.

R15.consumer_protection_notes: Account holder is deceased. Return benefits payments. Estate notification may be required.

R16.consumer_protection_notes: CRITICAL: If frozen for OFAC compliance, originating bank must treat as potential sanctions match. Do not re-originate. Contact BSA/Compliance.

R17.consumer_protection_notes: Format error in ACH record. Originator must correct and re-originate.

R20.consumer_protection_notes: Regulation D prohibits debiting this account type for this transaction class.

R21.consumer_protection_notes: Company identification (Originator Company ID) does not match RDFI records.

R22.consumer_protection_notes: Individual ID number does not correspond to a receiver account.

R23.consumer_protection_notes: Receiver has refused this credit entry. Originator should contact receiver.

R24.consumer_protection_notes: RDFI identifies this as a duplicate of a previously received entry. May trigger Reg E duplicate transaction dispute.

R29.consumer_protection_notes: Corporate (non-consumer) equivalent of R10. Debit not authorized by corporate customer. 60-day window. Note: Reg E does not apply to corporate accounts but Nacha unauthorized protections do.

R31.description: Permissible Return Entry (CCD and CTX Only)
R31.consumer_protection_notes: RDFI-permitted return for CCD/CTX entries for any reason within 2 banking days.

R33.consumer_protection_notes: Destroyed check entry return. Extended 60-day window applies.

R37.consumer_protection_notes: Original check (source document) was separately presented for payment alongside the ACH conversion. Double-payment situation. Extended 60-day window.

R38.consumer_protection_notes: Consumer placed stop payment on the original check that was converted to ACH. Extended 60-day window.

R39.description: Improper Source Document / Source Document Presented for Payment
R39.consumer_protection_notes: Check conversion entry did not comply with Nacha rules for check conversion. Extended 60-day window.

R51.description: Item Related to RCK Entry is Ineligible or RCK Entry is Improper
R51.consumer_protection_notes: Re-presented check (RCK) entry is improper or ineligible. Extended 60-day window.

R61.consumer_protection_notes: Return was routed to the wrong ODFI. Administrative correction needed.

R67.consumer_protection_notes: Return entry is a duplicate. ODFI received two returns for same entry.

R69.consumer_protection_notes: One or more mandatory fields contain invalid data.

R70.description: Permissible Return Entry Not Accepted / Return Not Requested by ODFI
R70.consumer_protection_notes: ODFI did not request this return and does not accept it.

R77.consumer_protection_notes: Administrative return dispute between ODFI and RDFI.

C01.correction_required: Originator must update account number with corrected data provided in NOC

C02.correction_required: Originator must update routing number with corrected data provided in NOC

C03.description: Incorrect Routing Transit Number and DFI Account Number
C03.correction_required: Both routing number and account number must be updated

C04.description: Incorrect Individual Name / Receiving Company Name
C04.correction_required: Originator must update the name field in future entries

C05.correction_required: Originator must change the transaction code (e.g., personal checking vs. savings)

C06.description: Incorrect DFI Account Number and Incorrect Transaction Code
C06.correction_required: Both account number and transaction code must be corrected

C07.description: Incorrect Routing Transit Number, DFI Account Number, and Transaction Code
C07.correction_required: All three fields must be updated

C08.correction_required: Routing number used for truncated entry must be corrected

C09.correction_required: Individual identification number must be corrected in originator records

REG_E_PROVISIONAL_CREDIT.description: Provisional credit must be issued to consumer within this window from when complaint is received

extended_window.condition: New account (< 30 days) or foreign-initiated transaction or point-of-sale transaction

REG_E_INVESTIGATION.description: Reg E dispute investigation must be completed within this window

extended_window.condition: POS transaction, foreign-initiated transaction, or new account (< 30 days)

NACHA_UNAUTHORIZED_RETURN.description: Unauthorized ACH return must be initiated within this window from settlement date

NACHA_STANDARD_RETURN.description: Standard ACH return window from settlement date

CTR_FILING.description: Currency Transaction Report must be filed with FinCEN within this window

SAR_FILING.description: Suspicious Activity Report must be filed with FinCEN within this window of detecting suspicious activity

extended_window.condition: Law enforcement contact — may delay up to 60 days if law enforcement requests

OFAC_REPORT.description: OFAC blocking / rejection report must be submitted within this window of blocking action

WIRE_FRAUD_RECALL.description: Wire recall request has best recovery chance within this window. After 24-48 hours, mule account may have been emptied.
WIRE_FRAUD_RECALL.regulatory_basis: Fedwire Operating Circular, NIST best practices
